page: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system includes 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in several areas. The instance achieves top-tier (top 10%) results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, software compilation, image processing, and text processing. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng, memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached read-only blocks, and Passmark database operations fall within the strong (top 25%) category. However, write bandwidth for large uncached blocks and single-core stress-ng performance remain in the average (middle 50%) tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8i.16xlarge offers high memory density and DDR5 speeds, which reduces the cost-per-GB of memory for memory-bound workloads. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for disk-heavy operations. The instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale Redis deployments, database operations, software compilation, and medium-to-large LLM inference prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 7 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per core. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics, software compilation, and Redis SET operations, alongside strong database operation capabilities. Conversely, write bandwidth for large uncached memory blocks and single-core stress-ng performance are average. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and LLM inference prompt processing, offering high memory density as a qualitative cost benefit despite the tradeoff of relying entirely on network-attached storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier Redis and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density DDR5 memory-optimized platform featuring 64 vCPUs and advanced Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive database and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C at 3.9 GHz
512.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier benchmark performance in Redis SET operations and software compilation
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and LLM inference
